Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed change to the POLARIS pipeline. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel if needed.

CHANGE: Question-driven scope section (sectoral coverage) via the facet skeleton (C5)
FILE/SEAM: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:1003-1013 (_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM), gates at :947-996 (PG_FACET_OUTLINE, PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON, both default OFF); _parse_outline already accepts required_sections (:1773) :: Comprehensiveness carries an industry-scope criterion (0.25 of the 0.29 dimension) and the task prompt itself says 'affecting various industries' — we ship ZERO industry-organised content. The facet skeleton that could host it exists and is dark.
WHAT: Arm PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 + PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1, plus a new gated addendum PG_FACET_SCOPE_DIMENSION (default OFF) appended to _FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM: 'If the research question names a scope dimension (industries/sectors, regions, populations, time periods), include exactly ONE thematic body organized ALONG that dimension, with one H3 per sub-scope the evidence genuinely covers; assign each sub-scope its supporting evidence IDs; never fabricate a sub-scope the evidence cannot ground.' Question-driven, no hardcoded topic — generalizes across the 100 tasks because it keys off the task's own text, avoiding the overfit trap named in the brief.
CLAIMS: +0.8 to +1.5 overall. This is the single largest zero-to-one criterion: we currently score a structural zero on a 0.25-weight comprehensiveness criterion. on comprehensiveness (0.29; industry-scope criterion) + instruction-following (0.25; 'various industries' is a graded instruction) (w=1.2)
FAILURE MODE THEY ADMIT: (a) Evidence pool may lack sector-tagged rows — the section renders thin or gap-stubs, and a gap stub on a prominent section is worse than absence; the outline-only test catches thin assignment before compose. (b) Flipping the whole outline to facet mode changes EVERY section title simultaneously — a confounded A/B; must be measured as its own arm. (c) Facet mode is the least-exercised outline path; parser fallback to generic-6 silently discards the whole change (detectable in the outline log).
NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
THE PRIOR NEGATIVE + NOISE: effect must exceed a +/-0.016 noise floor at n=1. Is the claimed gain distinguishable? And is this genuinely different from the STEP-3 insight directive that already failed, or is it the same idea with new words?

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# O1 (I-deepfix-001 #1344 — facet-driven outline): the live non-clinical outline was
# HARD-CAPPED to a fixed 6-title generic allow-list (Background / Key Findings / Evidence
# and Analysis / Comparative Assessment / Implications / Limitations) AND truncated to 6
# sections. Every distinct facet of a 20-facet question collapsed into 6 generic buckets —
# the container the F/D coverage workstreams all hit. This flag UNLOCKS the container for
# the NON-clinical path only: section TITLES and COUNT emerge from the evidence's real facet
# structure (one topical section per distinct facet the evidence supports), bounded only by
# real evidence-bearing facets — never a target. §-1.3: the truncate-to-6 is a CAP being
# REMOVED, not a knob being added; breadth EMERGES. Clinical/unknown stay byte-identical
# regardless of this flag (the proven Efficacy/Safety/... set governs clinical safety).
# DEFAULT-OFF (byte-identical) — the beat-both run config turns it on. LAW VI: env-tunable,
# read at CALL time so it is unit-testable via monkeypatch.
_FACET_OUTLINE_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE"
_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS"
_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS"
# Compute-safety ceiling ONLY (NOT a quality target, §-1.3): protects against a pathological
# outline that emits hundreds of near-duplicate facet titles (a section is billed per title).
# Generous; keyed to real evidence-bearing facets. Hitting it does NOT fail the outline (the
# facet plan is kept, not collapsed to the generic-6 fallback).
_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT = 40
# Below this many VALID facet sections the outline is treated as an EMPTY decomposition and
# the caller falls through to the generic-6 fallback. Default 1: a single well-grounded facet
# section is accepted (breadth emerges); 0 valid sections => generic-6 fallback. NEVER a floor
# that pads a thin outline up to a count.
_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT = 1


def _facet_outline_enabled() -> bool:
    """DEFAULT-OFF flag read at call time (monkeypatch/env-testable). ON widens ONLY the
    non-clinical outline to topical facet sections; clinical/unknown is byte-identical."""
    return os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
    )


def _facet_outline_active_for_domain(domain: str | None) -> bool:
    """True iff the facet-driven outline should govern THIS report: flag ON AND the domain is
    non-clinical (clinical/unknown always keep the proven fixed section set)."""
    if not _facet_outline_enabled():
        return False
    return str(domain or "").strip().lower() not in ("", "clinical")


# GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (mission STEP 2 — synthesis-enabling, topic-DRIVEN structure).
# The bare facet outline emits a flat BAG of thematic sections with no intro, no cross-study
# synthesis, and no conclusion/gaps — the exact SHALLOW-STRUCTURE defect that costs INSIGHT and
# COMPREHENSIVENESS on RACE. This flag wraps the emergent thematic facets in the standard
# research-report skeleton (intro/overview -> thematic bodies -> cross-study synthesis &
# contradictions -> conclusions & research gaps). It is a GENERAL structural improvement that
# applies to ANY non-clinical topic (AI/labor, finance, ecology, ...); it hardcodes NO topic,
# NO benchmark prompt, and NO thematic title (those still EMERGE from the evidence facets). The
# four structural ROLES are the only fixed part — a standard scholarly report skeleton. Read at
# CALL time (env/monkeypatch-testable). DEFAULT-OFF => the bare facet prompt (byte-identical).
_FACET_SKELETON_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON"


def _facet_skeleton_enabled() -> bool:
    """DEFAULT-OFF flag (read at call time). ON wraps the facet outline in the general
    research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusion+gaps).
    Only takes effect where facet mode is already active (non-clinical); clinical stays untouched."""
    return os.getenv(_FACET_SKELETON_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
    )


# The skeleton addendum appended to OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET when _facet_skeleton_enabled().
# Structure-ONLY: it constrains the ORDER and the presence of four structural roles; the thematic
# body TITLES and COUNT still emerge from the evidence. Topic-agnostic by construction.
_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM = """

GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (structure the plan as a COMPLETE report, not a flat bag of topics):
Order the sections so the report reads as a coherent scholarly review with these FOUR structural roles present in EVERY report, regardless of topic:
  1. OPEN with exactly ONE framing/overview section that orients the reader to the question, the scope, and the body of evidence (a good title names the subject, e.g. "Introduction and Scope of the Evidence" or "Overview of <subject>"). Assign it broad, high-level or foundational evidence.
  2. THEN the THEMATIC BODY sections — one per distinct facet the evidence genuinely supports (as instructed above). These titles EMERGE from the evidence; there is no fixed menu and no fixed count.
  3. Include exactly ONE cross-cutting SYNTHESIS section that reads ACROSS the thematic bodies and surfaces where studies AGREE, where they CONTRADICT each other, and how strong/consistent the overall evidence is (e.g. "Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions"). Assign it the evidence whose findings converge or conflict.
  4. CLOSE with exactly ONE section covering CONCLUSIONS and open RESEARCH GAPS (e.g. "Conclusions and Research Gaps"): what the evidence collectively supports, and what remains unresolved or under-studied.
The four ROLES (open / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps) are REQUIRED; the thematic-body titles are free to emerge from the evidence. Each section — including the four structural ones — still needs at least 2 genuinely supporting evidence IDs; assign the relevant rows, never fabricate a section the evidence cannot ground."""


def _facet_outline_max_sections() -> int:
    """Compute-safety ceiling for the facet outline (read at call time). Not a target."""
    try:
        v = int(os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_ENV, _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        v = _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT
    return v if v > 0 else _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT


def _facet_outline_min_sections() -> int:
    """Minimum VALID facet sections before falling through to the generic-6 fallback (read at
    call time). Floored at 1 — a single grounded facet section is a valid emergent outline."""
    try:
        v = int(os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_ENV, _FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        v = _FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT
    return v if v >= 1 else 1

def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.

    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""

    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)

A hotter lexical router would smear captcha + climate-policy rows into thematic sections, and **strict_verify would NOT stop them** (it blocks unfaithful sentences, not off-topic FAITHFUL ones) — it would make the report worse.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:16:  - Deeper causes I missed: (i) the magnet is the **tie-break**, not title length — `overlap > best_overlap` (vc:3864-3866) hands every tie to plan[0] = Introduction; (ii) **plan-side enrichment (my alternative) is decisively WORSE** — simulated, it routes 285/545 into Introduction, zero-overlap drops to 0, quarantine destroyed, and it routes literal "Just a moment..." pages into thematic sections; (iii) normalization (Jaccard) does NOT fix the magnet, it just moves it (GenAI/Conclusions become the new attractors, still on 1-word junk matches); (iv) `BasketMember` has **no title field** (credibility_pass.py:1504-1526), so the richer key REQUIRES threading `evidence_pool` in; member `direct_quote` is deliberately empty fail-closed and only folded for SUPPORTS members, so **row title+statement from the pool is the high-value addition**, not quotes.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:18:  - GATED SPEC for Rank3 (what will actually land): basket key += member row **title+statement** resolved from `evidence_pool`; scorer = raw overlap with **min_overlap >= 2** (mirrors the module's own `_DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP = 2`, vc:3384), tie-break overlap/|pw| then section order; **Intro / Cross-Study / Conclusions excluded as orphan TARGETS** (connective sections must not absorb primary evidence — note `_is_scaffolding_section_title` does NOT cover them, it matches render scaffolding only, so a new predicate is needed); residual keep-all + judge-only delete leg byte-unchanged; new kill-switch default-OFF. Honest expectation: **~100-150 rows correctly rehomed, ~400 stay in residual** — that remainder is the true state of this corpus.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:61:- 2026-07-12 21:40 | GROUNDING READ of baseline_routed_outline.json (8 sections, 1032 routed rows) — **THE CONFOUND IS NOW MECHANICAL, NOT SPECULATIVE.** Cross-referencing the prior armed judge's verdicts (outputs/agentic_report_20260712_203630/topic_judge_dispositions.json: n_in=997, n_off_subject_deletable=**507 = 51%**) against the routed outline shows the off-subject rows are **NOT quarantined in the catch-all — they are spread through EVERY real section**: sec1 Introduction 50.0% off-subject, sec2 Historical Patterns 57.3%, sec5 Polarization/Wages 50.0%, sec7 Conclusions 55.2%, sec8 "Additional Corroborated Findings" 57.2%; only sec3 (Occupational Exposure, 13.4%) is genuinely clean. **So the section writer composes its fixed 10-18 sentences from a menu that is ~half alien, strict_verify correctly kills the alien-derived sentences, and THAT is what kept_fraction=0.35 is.** This is exactly the confound the ceiling argument rests on, now with a per-section number. content.content:
TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.
- [w=0.15] Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: Evaluates if the review synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles), covering the main research themes, findings, and ongoing debates regarding AI's impact on the labor market. This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

=== INSIGHT (weight 0.32) ===
- [w=0.25] Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms :: Assesses if the review moves beyond superficial descriptions of labor market changes to deeply analyze the underlying *mechanisms* (e.g., task automation, skill augmentation, job creation/destruction dynamics, organizational adaptation) through which AI specifically reshapes job roles, skill requirements, and overall labor market structures.
- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
- [w=0.15] Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Measures how effectively the review articulates and leverages the 4IR framework to provide a deeper, more contextualized understanding of the *nature, scale, and interconnectedness* of AI's transformative power on the labor market, rather than treating AI's impact in isolation or merely mentioning the 4IR superficially.
- [w=0.20] Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives :: Assesses whether the review transcends simple summarization to identify and clearly articulate overarching emergent themes, significant theoretical linkages between concepts, or potentially novel perspectives derived from the synthesis of the reviewed literature on AI's labor market impact.
- [w=0.15] Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas :: Evaluates the review's capacity to distill strategically valuable implications (e.g., for policy, education, workforce adaptation strategies) and to clearly delineate critical research gaps or promising future research agendas based on the comprehensive synthesis of the current state of knowledge.

=== INSTRUCTION_FOLLOWING (weight 0.25) ===
- [w=0.10] Adherence to 'Literature Review' Format and Purpose :: Assesses if the article is structured and executed as a literature review, meaning it synthesizes and discusses existing published research on the topic, rather than presenting new empirical findings or being solely an opinion piece.
- [w=0.20] Consistent Focus on 'Restructuring Impact of AI on the Labor Market' :: Evaluates whether the article's content remains consistently and primarily focused on AI's restructuring effects on the labor market, as per the core task instruction, without significant digressions into unrelated AI applications or general economic theory not tied to labor impacts.
- [w=0.15] Integration of 'AI as a Key Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution' Theme :: Checks if the review explicitly incorporates and discusses AI within the specific context of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, as mandated by the task's focus requirements.
- [w=0.15] Explicit Addressal of AI-Driven 'Significant Disruptions' in the Labor Market :: Assesses whether the review specifically addresses and elaborates on the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI in the labor market, fulfilling another key focus point of the task.
- [w=0.15] Coverage of AI's Impact on 'Various Industries' :: Evaluates if the review discusses the impact of AI on the labor market across a spectrum of 'various industries,' rather than limiting its scope to a single industry or providing only a generic overview, as per the instruction.
- [w=0.15] Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles (or meet a clear standard of 'high-quality' if defined contextually, e.g., leading journals), and that other forms of literature (e.g., books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs, non-peer-reviewed reports) are not cited, adhering to the 'only cites high-quality... journal articles' constraint.
- [w=0.10] Exclusive Citation of 'English-Language' Journal Articles :: Ensures that all journal articles cited in the review are originally published in English or are established English translations, strictly adhering to the language constraint for sources.

=== READABILITY (weight 0.14) ===
- [w=0.20] L1: Language Clarity, Precision, and Academic Tone :: Evaluates the accuracy and fluency of sentence construction, grammar, spelling, and punctuation. Assesses the precise, consistent, and appropriate use of technical terminology related to AI, labor markets, the Fourth Industrial Revolution, and specific industries. Ensures an objective, formal academic tone suitable for a literature review. For example, terms like 'skill-biased technical change' or specific AI techniques should be used correctly and, if necessary, briefly contextualized for a broader academic audience.
- [w=0.20] S1: Overall Structure and Logical Organization :: Assesses the coherence and logic of the review's macro-structure. This includes a clear introduction defining the review's scope, purpose, and organizational map; logically sequenced thematic sections with informative headings that reflect the facets of AI's impact on labor (e.g., by industry, skill level, type of disruption); and a conclusion that effectively synthesizes the main themes from the literature and perhaps points to gaps or future research directions.
- [w=0.15] S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions :: Evaluates if each paragraph focuses on a single, clear idea or theme derived from the literature, with sentences flowing logically within the paragraph. Assesses the effectiveness of transitional phrases, sentences, and logical connectors in linking ideas smoothly between paragraphs and across different sections of the review, ensuring a coherent narrative thread.
- [w=0.15] P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: Assesses how clearly, concisely, and accurately key arguments, findings, theories, and methodologies from the cited journal articles are presented. Evaluates the effectiveness of synthesizing information from multiple sources to build a coherent understanding of topics (e.g., AI's effect on job displacement vs. creation, or changes in skill demands across industries), rather than just serially summarizing individual papers. Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy.
- [w=0.10] D1: Clarity of Data/Evidence Referenced or Summarized :: Evaluates the clarity with which quantitative or qualitative data, empirical findings, or specific evidence from cited studies (e.g., statistics on automation rates, survey results on worker sentiment) are described, interpreted, or summarized within the review. If the review itself uses tables or figures to compile or synthesize information from sources (e.g., a summary table of key studies and their findings), assesses their clarity, labeling, and effectiveness in aiding comprehension.
- [w=0.10] F1: Formatting, Layout, and Visual Consistency :: Evaluates the use of consistent and professional formatting (e.g., font choice and size, spacing, margins, heading styles) that enhances readability. Assesses the overall visual layout for ease of navigation and reduced reader fatigue, including clear paragraph breaks. Also considers the visual consistency and unobtrusive integration of in-text citations and the clarity of the bibliography's formatting, which contributes to the academic presentation.
- [w=0.10] A1: Audience Adaptation and Explanation of Terms :: Assesses if the language, level of detail, and style of argumentation are appropriate for an academic audience interested in AI's impact on the labor market. Evaluates whether potentially ambiguous or highly specialized terms are sufficiently contextualized or briefly explained to ensure understanding by readers who may not be experts in every specific sub-domain discussed (e.g., specific AI algorithms or niche economic theories).
=== TASK ===
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

content.text:
I dug into the code, the run artifacts, and the measurement history. The proposal's central factual premise is wrong.

## 1. The facet skeleton is NOT dark — it has been armed in every measured run since 2026-07-12

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:516-517`:

```python
os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")
os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")
```

The module-level default in `multi_section_generator.py` is OFF, but the production compose driver — the one that produced every flywheel arm (its field set matches `outputs/rank12_tierfirst_compose/compose_summary.json` exactly) — overrides it. Proof from the shipped report's own headings: `Introduction and Scope of the Evidence / …thematic bodies… / Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions / Conclusions and Research Gaps` — verbatim the `_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM` roles, not the generic-6 allow-list and not the clinical set.

This was STEP 2, and its gain (+0.129 RACE) was banked eight arms ago. So:
- "Arm PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 + PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1" is a **no-op**.
- Their own failure mode (b) — "flipping the whole outline to facet mode changes EVERY section title simultaneously" — describes a flip that already happened.
- Part of the claimed +0.8/+1.5 is credit for something already inside the baseline.

The residual novel content is **one sentence appended to a prompt string.**

## 2. Under the lens: the claim is smaller than the noise floor, by construction

Rank9 vs Rank10 are the same effective config (the theme floor promoted zero sections) → measured gap **0.0158 = the noise floor at n=1**. The claim is +0.8 to +1.5 (i.e. +0.008–0.015 RACE). **The entire claimed interval sits at or below one noise width.** The top of their range equals the whole night's measured gain (0.4052 → ~0.42). Not distinguishable at n=1 — and FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS's standing 03:00 directive is explicit: fix the instrument (k=5, SE~0.008), one bundled arm, ship only if ≥ +0.010. This proposes another n=1 arm below that bar.

## 3. Is it the same idea as the failed STEP-3 directive? Materially, yes

STEP-3 (`PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE`) was: inject one content directive into the outline/section prompt asking a detected section to organize content a certain way. Result: mechanism **fired as designed**, and RACE fell **-0.0353 overall, worse on every axis**, because the directive perturbed the live react loop → different outline, different pool, 588 fewer words. The proposed addendum perturbs the **same** live outline prompt in the **same** way (and with `PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1` every basket re-routes). The closest prior on this exact seam produced a negative twice the size of the claimed positive.

## 4. The "structural zero" is asserted, not measured — and the evidence isn't there

- The report already names healthcare, manufacturing, retail, finance, transport, agriculture, legal, customer support, software (47 industry/sector mentions in 7,829 words). No per-criterion RACE scores exist anywhere in the repo — "we score a structural zero" is an unmeasured assumption. A partial-credit criterion moving to "one organized section" is a fraction of the +0.055 theoretical ceiling, i.e. noise.
- Corpus check (997 rows): the top "sector" hit is `mining` = 81, almost all *data/text mining* false positives. Genuine sector rows are mostly **incidental mentions inside general papers** (Acemoglu–Restrepo matches on "manufacturing"), tier-skewed T3/T4/T6 (Brookings et al.).
- **Rank10 already ran this experiment.** The corpus-derived theme floor armed at seed=9 and promoted **zero** sections: every salient theme was already covered; the uncovered clusters were 169 rows of writing/students/marketing and 54 rows of legal/paralegal — *the alien rows the topic judge deletes* (566 junk rows deleted in Rank12). Commit e87f52e: **"'more sections' is EXHAUSTED on this corpus."** There is no latent sector cluster to host.
- The outline agent already searched "impacts across industries" and "robot industries" across 3 of its react turns (step5 per-turn table) and *still* produced no industry-organised section — the returned evidence does not decompose that way.

## 5. It collides head-on with Rank12, the cleanest win of the night

Rank12's source-eligibility firewall restricts the writer's menu to T1–T3 and bought **+29.5 points of T1-T3 citation compliance (43.3% → 72.8%)** — i.e. the instruction-following criterion *"Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles'" (w=0.15)*. Sector-specific material in this corpus is T4/T6 (industry/consulting) — exactly what the firewall demotes. So the sector section must either (a) pull T4-T6 rows and regress the w=0.15 criterion Rank12 just won, to chase the w=0.15 "various industries" criterion — a wash at best; or (b) stay T1-T3, in which case each sector H3 restates the same general findings under a sector label — the documented "verified-but-vacuous restatement" padding (30-35% of added words), which a judge grading "sector-specific nuances" will not reward.

## 6. Bonus: the faithfulness contract is unrelated boilerplate

The Tier-F/Tier-I contract describes `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER` (a second judge call per NEUTRAL sentence) — nothing to do with an outline-prompt addendum, which cannot admit a single new sentence class. If it is actually bundled in, that's a second confound and a second unmeasured arm on the same n=1 run.

**Verdict: refuted.**

Do not ship as proposed. If the industry-scope hypothesis is to be tested at all, it must be restated honestly and instrumented first:

1. DROP the two flag-arming claims entirely — PG_FACET_OUTLINE and PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON are already ON in every measured arm via compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:516-517. The arm is exactly one prompt sentence (PG_FACET_SCOPE_DIMENSION); re-price the claim accordingly.

2. MEASURE THE PREMISE BEFORE BUILDING. Score the existing Rank12 report per-criterion (the RACE harness can emit criterion-level scores) and confirm "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" (w=0.25 of comprehensiveness) is actually near-zero rather than mid-scoring. If it is not near-zero, the headroom is noise and the arm is dead on arrival — cost: one judge call, zero code.

3. RUN THE OFFLINE OUTLINE-ONLY TEST FIRST (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE exists, commit 0e0eca9): with the addendum on, does the outline produce a scope-dimension section whose H3s each carry >= 2 T1-T3 supporting rows? Rank10's theme floor and my corpus scan both say no — the sector-taggable rows are T4/T6 or judge-deleted junk. If the H3s cannot be filled from T1-T3, STOP: the section either gap-stubs or forces T4-T6 rows onto the menu and regresses the w=0.15 journal-tier criterion that Rank12 just bought +29.5 points on.

4. IF AND ONLY IF steps 2-3 both pass, obey the standing 03:00 directive: score it as a PAIRED k=5 arm (SE ~0.008) over frozen evidence assignments, ship only if delta >= +0.010. A single n=1 run cannot see an effect of the claimed size against the measured 0.0158 noise floor.

5. Unbundle the Tier-I inference contract — it is a separate lever with its own cost (a second judge call per NEUTRAL sentence) and belongs to a different arm.
